description: The current compiler2 closure-call failures on fz2-run and fz2-build are symptoms of a lossy native handoff: callable facts are computed strongly in the fact/artifact pipeline, then collapsed into constructor candidate vectors, mixed capture+arg ABI vectors, and block-local direct-target side tables that native codegen must reconstruct. Fix this at the model boundary. Closure captures are not call arguments. Publish first-class callable boundary contracts, carry exact direct-target closure-call facts explicitly, and make native codegen consume those contracts directly. This umbrella ticket closes the closure_typed_captures / curried_add / multi_caller_spec_divergent regression family by removing callable-constructor body guessing and mixed capture-prefix ABI reasoning.
title: Rebuild compiler2 closure-call boundaries around first-class callable contracts
